requirementGroups.title: ADDITIONAL APPROVED COURSES FOR THE MAJOR
ADDITIONAL APPROVED COURSES FOR THE MAJOR

notes.content: Preference will be given to junior-level applicants with the highest grades overall who have satisfactorily completed the following required courses. Required for admission : Students must have a cumulative UC transferable GPA of 3.0 (3.4 for TAG) and a cumulative GPA of 3.0 in required courses One year of approved calculus with a minimum GPA of 3.0 One year of calculus-based physics with laboratory for engineering and physics majors with a minimum GPA of 3.0 (Completion of two semesters of Physics fulfills the admissions requirement for the major, however, completion of the entire sequence before transfer is highly preferred) Concentrations are offered in: Biomedical Physics, and Engineering Physics Additional information is available at http://ps.uci.edu/undergraduates . Important: Completion of two semesters of Physics and two semesters of calculus fulfills the admissions requirement for the major, but completion of the entire sequence of Physics and all Math before transfer is highly preferred. Time to degree : Students who do not have the entire sequence of Physics and all Math completed before transfer will likely need 3 years to complete remaining degree requirements. NOTE : In fulfillment of the requirements below, a single course may be used only once. For information regarding the AP and IB examination credit policies refer to the UCI General Catalogue. For more information regarding the UC Irvine Transfer Admission Guarantee Program, please visit TAG
notes.contentHtml:
Preference will be given to junior-level applicants with the highest grades overall who have satisfactorily completed the following required courses.


Required for admission:

Students must have a cumulative UC transferable GPA of 3.0 (3.4 for TAG) and a cumulative GPA of 3.0 in required courses

  • One year of approved calculus with a minimum GPA of 3.0                                        
  • One year of calculus-based physics with laboratory for engineering and physics majors with a minimum GPA of 3.0 (Completion of two semesters of Physics fulfills the admissions requirement for the major, however, completion of the entire sequence before transfer is highly preferred)


Concentrations are offered in:

  • Biomedical Physics, and                                                      
  • Engineering Physics


Additional information is available at http://ps.uci.edu/undergraduates.


Important: Completion of two semesters of Physics and two semesters of calculus fulfills the admissions requirement for the major, but completion of the entire sequence of Physics and all Math before transfer is highly preferred.


Time to degree: Students who do not have the entire sequence of Physics and all Math completed before transfer will likely need 3 years to complete remaining degree requirements.


NOTE: In fulfillment of the requirements below, a single course may be used only once.


For information regarding the AP and IB examination credit policies refer to the UCI General Catalogue. 


For more information regarding the UC Irvine Transfer Admission Guarantee Program, please visit TAG
